page: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 16 shared vCPUs mapped from 8 physical AMD EPYC cores running at 2.0 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, featuring hyper-threading and hardware-assisted virtualization. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 3840 GB NVMe SSD. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average to strong tiers. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, ranking in the top 25% with a score of 2912.97. Multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores fall into the middle 50% average category, with scores of 46204.81 and 31969.29 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, yielding 123.94 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 70.41 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks also demonstrate average throughput, with Redis handling 28.04 million operations per second.

The shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, non-burstable compute performance, though the high memory capacity and large NVMe SSD storage offset this by offering high resource density. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the generous 128.0 GB memory allocation and 3840 GB NVMe SSD storage bundle make this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. This server is well-suited for database hosting, memory-cached web serving, large-scale file processing, and moderate LLM inference tasks.
description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC processor at 2.0 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, the server delivers strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. The 8.0 GB of memory per core and substantial local storage make this configuration highly resource-dense. However, the shared CPU allocation is a tradeoff for workloads requiring continuous maximum compute. This server is best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and web serving.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is a memory-optimized server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for databases and caching.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd features 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining a high-capacity NVMe SSD with strong single-core performance for database and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
3840 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage bundled
